title: OpenClaw in Your Hand: Building a Physical AI Terminal - Lech Kalinowski, Callstack
summary: Vault is a handheld AI terminal built on an ESP32-S3 with dual displays (OLED and e-paper) that runs local language models and autonomous agents. The system uses NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM to serve a 120B parameter model locally, enabling shell, assist, control, and game modes without cloud dependencies. The architecture separates firmware from a Python backend that dispatches to local models and agents, displaying real-time reasoning on e-paper.
writeup: This talk from Callstack's Lech Kalinowski covers Vault, a handheld AI terminal built on an ESP32-S3 with dual displays (an OLED and an e-paper screen) that runs local language models and autonomous agents. It serves a 120B parameter model locally via NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM, supporting shell, assist, control, and game modes with no cloud dependency. The architecture splits firmware from a Python backend that dispatches requests to local models and agents, with real-time reasoning shown on the e-paper display. The build illustrates how local inference, agent orchestration, and edge deployment combine in a physical device.
